children.heading: Caregiver
children.label: 1 Caregiver
children.text: The term “caregiver” means an individual who provides a service directly to an eligible child on a person-to-person basis.

children.heading: Child care certificate
children.label: 2 Child care certificate
children.text: The term “child care certificate” means a certificate (that may be a check or other disbursement) that is issued by a State or local government under this subchapter directly to a parent who may use such certificate only as payment for child care services or as a deposit for child care services if such a deposit is required of other children being cared for by the provider. Nothing in this subchapter shall preclude the use of such certificates for sectarian child care services if freely chosen by the parent. For purposes of this subchapter, child care certificates shall not be considered to be grants or contracts.

children.label: A
children.text: a child with a disability, as defined in section 602 of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ();20 U.S.C. 1401

children.label: B
children.text: a child who is eligible for early intervention services under part C of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.);20 U.S.C. 1431

children.label: C
children.text: a child who is less than 13 years of age and who is eligible for services under ; andsection 794 of title 29

children.label: D
children.text: a child with a disability, as defined by the State involved.

children.label: A
children.text: who is less than 13 years of age;

children.label: B
children.text: whose family income does not exceed 85 percent of the State median income for a family of the same size, and whose family assets do not exceed $1,000,000 (as certified by a member of such family); and

children.label: i
children.text: resides with a parent or parents who are working or attending a job training or educational program; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is receiving, or needs to receive, protective services and resides with a parent or parents not described in clause (i).

children.heading: English learner
children.label: 5 English learner
children.text: The term “English learner” means an individual who is an English learner, as defined in , or who is limited English proficient, as defined in .section 7801 of title 20section 9832 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is licensed, regulated, or registered under State law as described in ; andsection 9858c(c)(2)(F) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: satisfies the State and local requirements, including those referred to in ;section 9858c(c)(2)(I) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a child care provider that is 18 years of age or older who provides child care services only to eligible children who are, by affinity or consanguinity, or by court decree, the grandchild, great grandchild, sibling (if such provider lives in a separate residence), niece, or nephew of such provider, if such provider complies with any applicable requirements that govern child care provided by the relative involved.

children.heading: Family child care provider
children.label: 7 Family child care provider
children.text: The term “family child care provider” means one individual who provides child care services for fewer than 24 hours per day, as the sole caregiver, and in a private residence.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 8 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given it in .section 5304(e) of title 25

children.heading: Lead agency
children.label: 9 Lead agency
children.text: The term “lead agency” means the agency designated or established under .section 9858b(a) of this title

children.heading: Parent
children.label: 10 Parent
children.text: The term “parent” includes a legal guardian, foster parent, or other person standing in loco parentis.

children.heading: Scientifically valid research
children.label: 11 Scientifically valid research
children.text: The term “scientifically valid research” includes applied research, basic research, and field-initiated research, for which the rationale, design, and interpretation are soundly developed in accordance with principles of scientific research.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 12 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Health and Human Services unless the context specifies otherwise.

children.heading: Sliding fee scale
children.label: 13 Sliding fee scale
children.text: The term “sliding fee scale” means a system of cost sharing by a family based on income and size of the family.

children.heading: State
children.label: 14 State
children.text: The term “State” means any of the several States, the District of Columbia, the Virgin Islands of the United States, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “tribal organization” has the meaning given it in section 5304() of title 25.l

children.heading: Other organizations
children.label: B Other organizations
children.text: Such term includes a Native Hawaiian Organization, as defined in section 4909(4) of title 20 and a private nonprofit organization established for the purpose of serving youth who are Indians or Native Hawaiians.1See References in Text note below.1
